items.situation: two students debating which library floor is best for studying

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, where do you usually study now? Third floor or basement?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Honestly, the third floor's gotten too noisy. They added group tables.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, really? I haven't been since last spring.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Try the basement. It's a silent zone, and there are private carrels by the windows.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Carrels by windows? That sounds perfect for my thesis work.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Just grab one before two. They fill up fast after that.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Got it. I'll head there tomorrow morning.

questions.stem: What does the man say has changed on the third floor?

options.A: It has become noisier than before
options.B: It has added new vending machines
options.C: It has limited hours for finals
options.D: It has lost its window seating area

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：男生明确说三楼变得太吵，因为加了小组讨论桌。B错误：对话从未提到自动售货机。C错误：对话没提期末考试期间时间受限。D错误：对话只提到地下室有窗户旁的隔间，没说三楼失去窗座。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do tomorrow morning?

options.A: Move her thesis work back upstairs as before
options.B: Reserve a group table for her thesis discussion
options.C: Head to the basement to find a private carrel
options.D: Continue studying in her dorm room as usual

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：女生最后说"明天早上我会去那里"，结合男生让她在两点前到地下室抢隔间，可推断她会去地下室找隔间。A错误：三楼正是被否决的选项，她不会回去。B错误：小组桌正是导致三楼吵闹的原因，她不会去预订。D错误：对话中没有讨论待在宿舍的方案。

items.situation: two friends planning to split a textbook cost for a chemistry course

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Did you see how much the chemistry textbook costs this semester? Almost two hundred dollars.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Yikes. I was thinking we could split a used copy and share notes through the term.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Actually, that could work. We have the same lab section anyway, so we'd be on the same pace.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Right, and the library has one on reserve too, in case we both need it the same night.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Good backup plan. Let's check the bookstore's used section after lunch today.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Sounds great. I'll bring my student ID for the discount.

questions.stem: What is the woman's main suggestion to the man?

options.A: Buy a brand-new copy from the campus bookstore directly
options.B: Share a used textbook and split the cost
options.C: Borrow detailed notes from a previous classmate
options.D: Sign up for a totally different lab section

questions.explanation: 正确答案B：女生明确提出"我们可以合买一本二手书然后共享笔记"。A错误：对话中没有提到全新书，反而是买二手。C错误：对话从未提及向以前的同学借笔记。D错误：他们已经在同一个实验班，不需要换班。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the library copy of the textbook?

options.A: It is currently checked out by another student all term
options.B: It can be borrowed for the whole semester at once
options.C: It is missing several important chapters in the book
options.D: It serves as a backup when their copy is taken

questions.explanation: 正确答案D：女生提到图书馆有一本作为备用，"以防我们两个同一晚都需要它"，所以图书馆的书是他们共享本不可用时的备用方案。A错误：对话没说图书馆的书被借走。B错误：对话没提整学期可借。C错误：对话从未提到缺章。

items.situation: two roommates deciding how to decorate their dorm for the holidays

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: So, are we doing anything for the dorm before break? Last year was so plain.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, I was thinking some string lights and a small fake tree from the dollar store.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that's cute. But weren't string lights banned in dorms because of the wiring issue?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hmm, you're right. The RA said battery-operated ones are okay though.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Perfect. I'll grab a set at the campus store tomorrow.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: And I'll figure out the tree situation this weekend.

questions.stem: What does the man originally suggest buying?

options.A: A large fabric wreath for the dorm door
options.B: A pair of decorative pillows from the bookstore
options.C: A string of lights and a small tree
options.D: A scented candle set for the study desks

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：男生说"我在想灯串和一棵小假树"，所以最初的建议就是灯串和小树。A错误：对话没提花环。B错误：男生提的是dollar store，不是bookstore，且没提枕头。D错误：对话从未提到香薰蜡烛。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about plug-in string lights?

options.A: They are not permitted in the dorm rooms
options.B: They are sold only at off-campus stores
options.C: They are too expensive for most students
options.D: They are difficult to install on dorm walls

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：女生提到灯串因为线路问题在宿舍被禁，男生确认"你说得对，电池式的才被允许"，可推断插电式灯串不被允许。B错误：对话没提销售地点。C错误：价格从未讨论。D错误：安装难度未涉及。
